decisions.rationale: BAC's 92.4 momentum score (highest among financials candidates), conf=7/10, reject=False, and Q2 earnings catalyst on July 16 align perfectly with RISK_ON/MOMENTUM regime while diversifying the existing financial sector exposure away from pure investment banking toward consumer/commercial banking with NIM stabilization thesis.

EQIX carries the lowest composite quant score (66.0) and lowest momentum (64.8) among current holdings with a bear case flagging hyperscaler vertical integration risk and value-trap characteristics; capital is better deployed into higher-momentum RISK_ON names given the current regime.

JPM (quant 69.1, hold=6/10) is the weakest financial holding with lowest momentum among the bank positions; selling frees capital while BAC (92.4 momentum) offers superior near-term alpha in the same sector with a more favorable earnings catalyst profile.

AAPL (quant 68.4, conf=6/10) adds LARGE_CAP_TECH exposure explicitly favored by the RISK_ON regime, with Q3 earnings on July 28 as near-term catalyst and Services-driven margin expansion providing quality anchor to balance the portfolio's high-beta financial and energy positions.
